_readme.purpose: Answer key for the EXTRACTION eval (scripts/extraction_eval.py). Each entry pins the correct STRUCTURE of one specific table in the indexed library, so we can measure whether find_tables / the reconstructors captured it faithfully. Turns each QA correction into a regression fixture — and, long-term, a labelled example for a learned source-material scanner (see the project memory on the extraction roadmap).
_readme.how_to_score: Find the cached table by `match_cells` (cell values unique enough to identify it), then check `expect_headers` against the header row, `expect_row_labels` against the first column of the data rows, and `min_rows`. A table is correct only if all three hold.
_readme.gate_vs_advisory: `known_broken: true` entries are advisory TODOs — tables we KNOW are currently mis-extracted; they're reported but don't gate, like edition_sensitive entries in the retrieval eval. The gate accuracy covers the rest, so the eval is a live regression guard for known-good tables from day one. When an extraction fix lands, flip `known_broken` to false so the table joins the gate.
_readme.local_only: Reads cache/*.json (the copyrighted books can't live in the repo), so it's a LOCAL guard like scripts/retrieval_eval.py. To validate an extraction fix: reindex with force (re-download + re-extract → updates the cache), then re-run the eval.

entries.title: Chance of Success, Pushed Roll
entries.what_was_wrong: FIXED (find-tables-lines-v11): find_tables(strategy=lines) dropped the bottom D12 attribute/skill row (no bottom ruling line). The D6/D8/D10/D12 columns are present but the D12 ROW was missing — captured 5 rows, should be 6. The trailing-row recovery pass (pdf_tables._recover_trailing_rows) now restores it: ['D12', '88%', '93%', '96%', '97%'].

entries.title: Chance of Success, Initial Roll
entries.what_was_wrong: FIXED (find-tables-lines-v11): same D12 bottom-row drop as the pushed-roll table on the same page (recovered row ['D12', '65%', '74%', '79%', '82%']). Its extracted title is still garbled ('shows the chance when pushing.') — title-only, the rows are now complete.

entries.title: Military Ranks
entries.what_was_wrong: Regression guard for a KNOWN-GOOD table — the full 18-rank US/Soviet/Polish/Swedish ladder that chargen promotions read. Fails if a find_tables change drops ranks or the bottom Lieutenant General row.
